div.head: The Consul at Geneva (Gilbert) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
190. In communication to the League the British Government states that upon being approached by the Paraguayan Government concerning the issuance of a license for the sale to Paraguay of two
aeroplanes “for purely ambulance purposes” it had replied that it could not allow such an order to be fulfilled in view of the fact that the proposed embargo covers the supply of aircraft of all types. The British Government expresses the hope that other governments will make the same reply if approached for the same purpose and suggests that the Committee of Three make a communication in this sense to the governments which have declared their willingness in principle to accede to the embargo.
